Great professor that I'd recommend everyone in ACC 311 take.

He gives extra credit! He is very approachable. The best accounting professor ever. He really broke concepts down for me in office hours, and appreciates effort. This was the first accounting course I had ever taken, and he teaches it so well that it was easy to grasp.

Fannin is the best teacher I've had in my time as a student. He has a wealth of knowledge and makes all his students really learn the info and takes away their ability to memorize it. This allows for applied knowledge beyond the course and great success on the test. Take him if you can.

I don't know what I am gonna get in this class, but Fannin is an all-time guy. Cares about his students. Brightens everyone's day even during an 8 am lecture. I could fail this class and still be happy I learned from him.

Professor Finley was great! This was her first semester teaching and her sections did better than others. She really cares that students understand what's going on and takes a lot of time out of her day to help you. I would def take her again and there was a curve.

Prof Finley really cares about her students and values learning over grades. The course material is difficult, but she does a good job on explaining and is always willing to meet outside of class time. TAKE HER! She is the best.

analysis.name: Teri Fickling

Can't recommend Dr. Fickling enough! It was an absolute privilege to be in her class this semester and out of all my semesters at UT, it's been my favorite. Teri is not only an incredible professor, but also an incredible human being. It is evident that she is very passionate and knowledgeable about the class materials. WOULD RECOMMEND 5/5

analysis.name: Katherine Grooms

A wonderful professor! She is passionate about what she teaches and does her best to make the class as engaging as possible, even with the large class size. took her last semester- DON'T TAKE THIS COURSE. v ez but time-consuming if want 2 put effort. 1semprjct 4 parts v manageable. You are LUCKY if you have the chance to take him. I was so scared to take Ochem bc I'm an average student and I loved his class and he is hands down my fav prof. Sarkesian seems to have graded harder than other classes, requires more class participation/attendance, has strict classroom policies, and expects theatrical performances over content in speaking assignments. There were opportunities for extra credit, but overall her grading on the presentations was tough and hard to please/receive an A.

Although she is a sweet person, her grading is extremely biased. She definitely plays favorites in class, and she never really tells you why she takes off points. She expects performances for her presentations and will grade you harshly if you are not enthusiastic enough. don't really have to study for tests, participation matters

This class should not have been as hard as it was. The grading is very unclear, and the TA's grade super unjust. The rubric was never clear, and for group presentations it does not matter how good your content is, but more about theatrics and giving her a show. Do not recommend.

analysis.name: Carley Law
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: If there's another professor for the class you need, take the other one. Her grading depends heavily on your relationship with her, plus you won't know your grade until the end of the semester when it is too late to do any changes or do better in the next projects. Very passive-aggressive with her emails if you even get one from her back.

She is a very tough grader and is very disrespectful. She does not care about the wellbeing of her students. She is very unclear about her projects and assignments and gets mad when students don't understand. She will embarrass you in front of the whole class and is very unencouraging.

Honestly kind and caring professor when it comes to critique and individual feedback. Though, does not hold herself to the same standards she puts onto her students. Pushed back deadlines, last minute project changes, passive-aggressive when it comes to discussing grades, and does not grade anything until the last minute. Sweet but unprofessional.

You only receive grades back at the very end of the semester. Even when she likes something in class, you may receive a poor grade on it and there's no way to know. She's extremely nit picky in her grading. When her TA told me they liked something, she would disagree but I would not know this until grades came out. The grading feels subjective.

Carley is an amazing professor. She is so passionate about what she does & you can tell she wants to connect w/ her students. We haven't received grades yet, but she offers PLENTY of opportunity to raise your grade by attending design events. I'm not sure why she has bad ratings? She's genuinely such an inspiration. It's not a hard class at all.

analysis.name: Robert Moser
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 230 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Moser literally reads off a teleprompter for all of the required lecture videos, but stutters on every other word. He is a bad lecturer and is unclear in both his slides and module videos. Overall, the class is a lot of work but not too bad. The professor, on the other hand is pretty god awful. Exams have poor word choice and bad grammar.

Lectures are sooo horrible because they literally read a teleprompter and stutter and struggle with their own words. It's hard to learn because of this. My eyes glaze over. Class/quiz is online only ONCE a week but there's a lot of videos you have to watch outside of class. Required readings are long but you don't actually need to read them.

Grades tough. If you do not put specifically what they want you to put then you might as well pencil yourself in as a B. Tests are way harder than they should be. TAs grade like you have to quote the textbook. Would not recommend. Should have taken community college Gov.

His online GOV 312L class is pretty awesome. I didn't pay attention sometimes, but for the most part his lectures are interesting, he includes current events and talks about debatable issues and we get to see where we are on the spectrum compared to the rest of the class. 2 tests and 1 paper. Pretty easy overall. Just go over the powerpoints.

Moser is an alright professor, sometimes the modules can be very dry and he stumbles on his words often. The class really wasn't that bad; as long as you keep up with the module videos and read the class google docs before a test, you will be fine. The people saying this is the hardest class they have taken haven't taken a STEM course at UT.

analysis.name: Patrick McDonald
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 152 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Lectures are boring and it's never clear on what you need to understand. The textbook assignments are so long and boring as well and it is one of the worst things ever. So many assignments due every week, take so long and overall DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS.

McDonald is a good professor. Watching him and Moser interact was always funny. Course material can be a bit difficult to understand and goes pretty fast at times. I always had a relatively hard time on the test, as they seemed a little hard for an intro course like this. Lots of outside the class modules and readings. Overall, I enjoyed the course

This class's exams were way harder than they needed to be for a core class. The modules were easy, it's fine, but they don't really provide you with any study materials for exams. The exams had like "A and B" and "A, B, and C" options that made it confusing and just harder than it needed to be. Idk, easy lectures, tough exams.

Exam grades make 75% of the grade in the class. Even if you take care of your assignments that covers only 25% of your grade. If you mess up one exam really bad then you are pretty much screwed for the class. I suggest that if you take this class you should be ready to block out multiple hours of your day outside of your main classes to study gov

Professors were super funny and effectively communicated content. I basically skimmed the textbook and just paid full attention to lectures, taking a lot of notes for understanding, and I did quite well on the exams especially with the curve. A lot of material but very interesting + informative. Several extra credit opportunities offered.

analysis.name: Michael Sailors
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 97 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Super easy VAPA if you just do the work. About 1, 5 question quiz a week and all the answers can be found word for word in the previous lecture. Majority of your grade comes from Cerego Homework which is so easy as long as you just do it. You are required to go to two jazz concerts a semester and write a 500 word paper but its graded super easy.

Cerego is a bit annoying but easy to complete. Lectures are 15- 30-ish minutes have 6-8 instapolls and there's like 6 per module and 4 modules per week. great guy, DO CEREGO AND LISTENING QUIZZES. Those will TANK your grade if you forget. Easy class jsut keep up

Honestly ended up finding the content very interesting, despite not really enjoying jazz prior to taking this class. Now, I really can appreciate jazz whenever I hear it! The lectures are great and interesting, and the Cerego sets are totally manageable as long as you don't start late. Really easy class that I really enjoyed.

Dr. Sailors was a great professor and made jazz fun and exciting. The part of going to concerts for a grade and writing about them was fun. Make sure you show up to every class for your instapolls there's a lot (100, graded for 90). Overall the class was very enjoyable.

Girl. Just take this class. You don't need to love jazz, but it's definitely an easy class. That said, it does come with work but it's nothing that should be giving you a headache. Only thing that may be bothersome to some people is to actually go out to a concert. I went to the free UT Jazz Combo nights at Mozarts and loved it.

analysis.name: Madhavi Mallapragada
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Lengthy lectures full of concepts that are not well-explained. The grade is test-heavy, and the tests were graded extremely harshly. Other than exams, you have four written assignments only worth 20 points total. A lot of outdated content from 20+ years ago.

Dr. Mallapragada's class may seem intimidating at first, but it was truly a wonderful survey of Asian Americans in media that I continue to reference past the end of the semester. Participation and speaking in class do matter, which is something she communicates to the class. However, an honest effort to try and read is all that is required.

Lectures were solid. Assignments are mainly reading responses that are heavily graded. The only feedback I got was essentially "write better", even on exams. I had a B+ in the course but because I missed more than 8 classes she took off 10% of my grade. Other than the terrible attendance policy this class is pretty good, just make sure to show up.

Professor Mallapragada's class was extremely boring to take, 2 hours of speaking endlessly without caring for student engagement. ZERO tolerance for absences, assigns pop quizzes, regardless of emergencies or illnesses. Class is crammed with ridiculous amounts of readings and unfairly timed tests, and random changes to her exam policies. AVOID!!

Dr. Mallapragada is one of the best professors in the AAS department! She is so considerate of student needs (she adjusted exams based on our feedback, and flexed homework due dates whenever possible). Her lectures are amazing: she's smart, funny, and articulate. Exams will be easy if you take good notes and participate regularly.

analysis.name: Robert Newberry
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 48 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: this man is the goat. I hope this dude wakes up in the morning knowing that this world is a better place with him in it. actually helps you learn stuff, gives very very fair tests, and he's just him in general. I hope bro has an ice cold pillow on both sides every night

Newberry was an amazing professor! Ochem is a hard class, but he made it almost "easy" to succeed. Use the old exams and hw's to study, bc the exam structures are identical. GO TO OFFICE HOURS (John's were amazing) they help sm with really breaking down the material. Also, dw abt the final being worth a lot, if you study you WILL do well! TAKE HIM!

My favorite prof ever! Lectures were very helpful, I really recommend going to every lecture you can- it's better in person imo. 3 exams but he drops one, and he tells you exactly how each exam will be/the type of questions, you know exactly what to study for- he never tricks you. Lots of hw/quizzes get dropped too, but each prepares you for exams.

Dr. Newberry is an excellent professor who actually makes Ochem interesting and simple to understand! His class is structured extremely well, and he provides plenty of resources outside of lectures to get your questions answered and help prepare you for the exams. One thing I will say is that I wish he did more practice questions during lectures!

By far one of the most enjoyable and one of my fav profs at UT. Newberry takes difficult material and breaks it down during lecture in a way that really helps you understand the underlying concepts behind reactions. Exams are all very fair and structured identically to the practice exams that have video explanations. Wish he was teaching OCHEM2

analysis.name: Nicolas Reyes
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 102 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Had some of the best reviews for a calc prof but his switch from online exams to in person broke this class. Quizzes are two questions completely free response and weighted almost identically to the midterms. They are super difficult and they are NOT curved. I finished 408C with a 98 and am struggling to do well on his quizzes.

The professor described the quiz on proofs of reductions as “easy,” many students who don't work with this material found it quite challenging. The topic was not clearly explained during the TA discussion, which made the quiz harder to prepare. I would not be surprised if the scores were curved. lectures are sporadic and are not clear.

Reyes style of teaching, syllabus, and just general vibes from him have changed. Maybe something having to do with new math department guidelines as he mentioned on our first day. The main issues are homework not correlating to lectures, discussions being overly hard, and just in general no synchronization between the many parts of the course.

This class feels completely inconsistent compared to last semester. Previously, exams were online and the course structure reflected that format. This semester, exams were suddenly moved to fully in-person, which felt like a major shift without adequate adjustment in teaching or preparation. Expectations changed, but the support did not.

I had high hopes for this class, but overall it was one of the most frustrating academic experiences I've had. The transition from online to fully in-person came halfway through the year with no clear explanation or advance warning. That change made planning around other classes and responsibilities much more difficult.

analysis.name: Alison Norman
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 162 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Just another typical UT CS professor. She cares more about whether her students are cheating or not then if they are understanding the material. Projects are hard, but not impossible if you give up on all of your social life. Test are easier than projects, but a lot of people don't study much because of the extremely long projects. Just work hard.

She cannot help much with the projects.  Tests are overly hard, made a day or so before giving them.  No clear grading rubric.  Homework is not checked, maybe waiting over a month for project grades, and you will go into tests not really knowing where you stand if you really grasp the concept.  Professor covers way too much information between test

If I could rate any lower, I would. The group projects are unnecessarily time-consuming and quite literally take a consistent daily effort of 4-5 hours. The tests always nitpick on the smallest detail mentioned on the slides. Dr. Norman is a nice person but that doesn't help the fact that her class is literally a full-time job. PintOS is just a no.

One of the worst professors at UT, not because of her teaching, but because of her grading system. The grading system is designed to make you fail. Also, lectures are pretty much useless as all the learning is done in pre-lecture videos (can take 1-2 hours). Projects are difficult and very time consuming, expect about 30-40 hours per week.

Norman is super nice and a great lecturer. She always asks if anyone has any questions at every opportunity. The reason the class is so difficult is 1) There is A LOT of material you have to know 2) The projects are LONG. 40 hours a week long. My only critique is her tests are garbage. Reviewing past tests is A MUST to understand grading criteria.

analysis.name: Nathan Stone
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Stone is a hilarious and interesting guy, this class is definitely tougher than other ugs but he makes it easier by giving lots of feedback and being very forgiving. he siad he'd cut back on readings this semester but everything that was required was immensely interesting and his experiences made the class very enjoyable

lots of reading, some difficult essays to write, but Dr. Stone is very caring and always gave detailed feedback, and guided class discussions when necessary. this class is a bit more work than other ugs, but Dr. Stone isn't a harsh grader. i feel like i learned a lot in this class, so i would recommend to anyone genuinely interested in the subject

Professor Stone is a great Spanish professor. He was one of the most inspirational professors I've had in Spanish as he actually made me want to put in extra effort to learn. This class does move really fast, I felt like we never had time to fully talk about grammar topics so be prepared for that. Do your work and talk to him, he's awesome

Dr. Stone is a very nice and funny guy, and his class was relatively easy; lenient and willing to work with you. A lot of assignments both inside and outside of class, but normal for a language class at UT. My only complaint is that sometimes lectures can feel very random, and at times I've left lectures not knowing what I should've taken away.

Stone is a very inspiring Spanish professor who cares about his students. He is extremely accommodating and laid-back, but he is still extremely knowledgeable about Spanish and doesn't reward laziness. He is very accommodating, too.

analysis.name: Nicole Butterbaugh
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment:
The most impressive part of this class is how simple material was turned into an unnecessarily harsh and mind-numbing course. Feedback didn't improve grades, studying didn't help exams, and missing class meant penalties with no leniency. 

The course could have been more manageable and engaging. Frustrating and poorly handled, I hope she eases up.

Do not take her if you have any other option. To say the least, the assignments were odd, with poor instructions, and poor explanations when you asked for clarification.  The only thing you could do for a test was pray, most of the topics on the tests we never covered. And the group project, all I can say is good luck.

Do NOT take this class. I thought it wasnt going to be that bad but it is. The lectures are so boring and there is SO much work. The tests are hard and she is not the nicest. Seriously take any other class. Especially if you know you already have a busy semester!

The material was easy to learn, and although the assignments did not have the clearest instructions, they were also pretty straightforward. When asking questions for help, she wasn't the most helpful or nicest, but she did her job I guess.
Rudest professor I've ever had. How do you teach a communications class but lack all communication and leadership skills?

analysis.name: Sean Neagle
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: AMAZING professor. One of my favorite classes I've ever taken (I'm a STEM major btw, so definitely not up my usual alley). Lectures were very engaging and genuinely interesting. 90% of your grade is based on the test, very easy if you attend lectures and pay attention. Reading does not affect your grade at all so don't bother.

I had this professor for my first year at UT Austin First, if you want to be successful in his class PAY attention . The three exams make up 90% of your grade so if you fall behind then your grade is pretty much cooked. However his gradings are fair. The test is based on lecture notes, no suprises. Genuinely good teacher, cares about his class

He's alright as a teacher. His 318 was a solid intro class. I like that he fostered discussions rather than yapping at us. I just don't like the fact that the entire class is based on three in-class essay exams because if you mess up one like I did, you're simply cooked.

analysis.name: Natalie Czimskey
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 72 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I LOVED taking Dr.Czimskey's class. She also has the "Longhorn Friday" where, if ALL class wears burnt orange on Friday, she will bring either treats next class or add extra points. She will also allow you to give burnt orange items to classmates before class. She is very funny during lecture and you have access to all ppt. on Canva.WILL take again

She is amazing as always, phonetics content is very overwhelming at first but makes sure her students fully grasp the info. Her and her TA are very easy to reach outside of class and will help you in anyways they can. She makes sure we get the printed out version of the lectures and I think it helps a lot more than typing it out on a computer!

sweetest professor ever! I look forward to her classes every week. Easy to get in contact with, inclusive with all students, explains material in a way that's easy to understand. Love going to office hours and just chatting with her! Would definitely take any and all of her classes again.

EXTREMELY caring and accessible prof. Attendance isn't mandatory. All tests are online and not monitored (she respects our privacy and trusts students not to cheat). I am not an SLHS major, but she made the class fun. Also, an optional final to replace the lowest test grade. Only con is that there's very few assignments outside of the tests.

Great option to take as a science core class, even if you aren't an SLH major. Dr. Czimskey is funny, engaging, and is truly passionate about her field. Make sure to take notes in class as the slides don't cover everything you need to know. Very helpful in office hours if any guidance is needed.

analysis.name: Mina Ogando-Lavin
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Don't skip class, because if you do you won't know what vocab she's quizzing you on in the next class. There is a homework assignment before every single class, but they are super easy. Her classes are very relaxed, and as long as you're speaking spanish she really doesn't care what you're talking about. She just sets you loose to chat all class.

Mina's a great professor, does her best to keep the class engaged and is very nice. The class itself was relatively chill, and you'll pass as long as you stay on top of your work. An A is very much possible if you study.

Condescending, rude. Have to buy a $150 workbook she wrote which you barely use. Grading isnt the best, besides exams and quizzes, some assignments are graded purely on how she feels about you. I could've gotten an A since my exam/quiz/homework grades were high, but I left with an A- because she felt like I wasnt a good student? Make it make sense!

I learned SO much in this class. It was a super organized/structured class, which I loved. There were a lot of assignments, but this kept me on track. I wouldn't say it's difficult to get a good grade in this class. I would turn in things late and Prof. Ogando was so accommodating. I would love to take another class with Prof. Ogando.

Sometimes she would make me feel dumb in class, and the way that the grammar rules are explained are very hazy. You have to rely on the homework to decipher what grammar rules you're learning in class, because it's very applied in the classroom. She has a lot of energy but doesn't facilitate the best learning environment

analysis.name: Shannon O'Brien
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 95 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Lets just throw together 2 tests worth most of your grade while giving a study guide each with 300 terms then calling it a day. The tests are written as if they're open note and with 300+ terms in each test, pray youre lucky and get easy ones, every mistake is costly and getting 2-3 more wrong is a letter grade difference. Terrible grading system

Anything you heard before Fall 2025 about this class IT HAS CHANGED!!! Tests are honorlock and you are expected to know over 300+ vocab terms for each test. In retrospect if you have the time every week to listen to 10 lecture vids each being 45 min you should be okay in the class but If you're looking for an easy A+ I wouldn't take this class.

She's funny... But that's all I really got. The each test is 27% of your grade. Involves memorizing over 300 words, CLOSED BOOK/NOTE. In my opinion, the exams seem to be formatted like they're open book/note (like they were previously), but the percentage it takes up in the class does not reflect the increased difficulty for students.
Tests are implemented so students suffer and make sure your grade stays down. Interesting lectures ig

Uses honorlock. Optional prerecorded lectures. Very light workload. For exams, lots of terms to memorize. If you watch all the lecture videos (3 hrs/wk) and pay attention, you'll do fine on exams. Little EC/No curve. Answers emails quickly (same with TAs)

analysis.name: Shannon O'Brien
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 113 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: I LOVED Shannon's class. Great intro professor, makes it interesting for all the non-majors. Very clear on grading, tells you exactly what to study on the tests. As long as you take good notes in class, do the assignments (super easy), and read the textbook, it's incredibly easy to get an A. Also, she's super helpful in office hours and very kind.

Dr. O'Brien is a cool and funny teacher. This class isn't hard. Just don't overthink her tests. She really doesn't try to trick you. I would recommend using quizlet to study! Go to her office hours. She's super chill and easy to talk to. I overall enjoyed this class and she made this subject intresting. Lastly, you don't have a final! Great teacher

ALL reviews before fall 25 are INVALID. The class changed and now has HonorLock proctored and closed note exams. There are only TWO exams for the whole semester each with about 400 terms. You can study for days, and still do bad on the exams, if you don't memorize every single term. Such a horrible class that you unfortunately have to take for CORE

Yk that scene from mean girls when she's writing in the book screaming and crying. That's me with this course. Exams require honorlock and you can't use any notes, despite being able to use them on assignments and quizzes. I'd rather she say we can't use notes on anything because why have I been writing stuff down just to not use them on exams???

The lectures are long, only to be followed by 5-10 question assignments that often have nothing to do with her lectures. Taking notes is close to pointless and time consuming. Midterm and final questions are pulled from a random test bank, so be prepared to memorize literally 300 terms, only to get a random 50. Tests are hard but assignments arent.

analysis.name: Yukie Okada
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently

review_highlights.comment: I took business Japanese with Okada Sensei, and although the content was challenging at times, she tries her best to help us understand. She is always very kind, has helpful office hours, and explains stuff in English when Japanese is difficult. Attendance is important and there are daily quizzes, but they aren't hard to study for. I recommend!

Okada Sensei is a very kind and understanding professor. She helped me ease back into Japanese after an 8-month hiatus and really pushed me to develop my speaking and listening skills. Her class is difficult, but if you really make an effort, it feels more like a fun challenge than anything else.

-1% off your final grade for every absence (3 dropped), there are lectures every day!! 3 late = 1 absence, even a second late will be counted!! Ridiculous, as if missing the daily quiz wasn't already a penalty don't take her there are way better professors

analysis.name: Diana Orozco-Lapray
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently

review_highlights.comment: Dr. O was such an amazing professor. At first, the exams can seem very daunting and the lectures seem fast paced. However, you get used to it and her lectures are very engaging. There are many extra credit opportunities and a chance to replace your lowest exam grade. Dr. O is so kind and understanding, and most of all deeply knowledgeable. TAKE HER

Dr.O is an amazing professor! Her class is one of the few that I made sure I attended despite her not taking attendance most days. Her lectures were engaging, fun, and interesting. Your grade is exam heavy, but she offers extra credit at the end of the year. Exams were tough but mostly everything is covered in lecture.

This professor is AMAZING. She is kind, loving, and treats her students like her own kids. Her class is normally paced, and the lab is one of my favorite things I've done in college. If you have the opportunity to, TAKE HER. SHE IS BEYOND INCREDIBLE!!!!

She is a very knowledgeable and kind professor. Her class is attendance optional. Test material comes entirely from lectures/recording. Other assignments are weekly quizzes/written assignments/activity. No test review but easy to study for just review notes/lecture recordings. She is very approachable for questions or office hours. I recommend her.

I loved Dr. O's class! So much wonderful info and very relevant to the world. She hasn't taught 313 in a while, so the class is still changing. 3-4 Exams + Lab portion with 2 easy papers! Lab grade is separate. Tests are open-note + online, but I would study for them. I learned a lot in this class! She wants you to do well! Incredibly knowledgeable

analysis.name: Ryosuke Okuno
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This professor is dismissive, unapproachable, and unsupportive. He rarely helps, lets his ego dominate, and speaks negatively about students behind their backs. Even though I earned an A, I would not recommend taking his class.
Lectures are disorganized and difficult to follow. He is unapproachable and often impatient when students ask questions during office hours, despite putting on a forced, insincere smile.
Barely learned anything. Lectures were messy and unprepared, and most of the time it was hard to tell what he was even trying to teach. Worst professor I have ever had.

analysis.name: John Osborn
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 76 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Everything (including some exams) is collaborative, essays are not bad, and the content is very manageable. If you can look at definitions/theorems and apply them, you can do the proofs in this class. He's a nice guy but particular about notation and formalities. Be respectful and you'll be fine. Probably the easiest class in the department.

GOAT! Tests are identical to assigned study problems (or similar methods to solve). 6 quizzes, 2 dropped. GO TO DISCUSSION!! HELPS A LOT FOR PREPARING FOR TESTS AND QUIZZES. No graded homework, but they were useful as practice for exams. 

I personally didn't think he was the best lecturer, but he makes the class easy if you put in a little effort.

Gives you almost, if not the exact questions that will be on the midterm. Midterms are five questions and he gives you 12 to study, which is extremely manageable. TA was fantastic and explained solutions very clearly. Lecture is a little theory heavy, but solutions to every homework are posted in a timely manner. Take him if you want an A.

Lectures are useless, but TA sections are GREAT. I didn't go to any lectures beyond the first week of class.

Prof gives a study guide before every exam, and the problems on the exam will typically be drawn straight from the study guide.

class is not an easy A but it is obtainable if you do not make mistakes, miss readings, homework, or presentations. You will have to teach yourself a lot .You will have to treat this class as a regular required class so be wary with how many other classes you are taking. He does offer extra credit opportunities and you will learn a lot in the end.

analysis.name: Youjeong Oh
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Oh is very smart, but not a good teacher. She also can make an lecture 2x longer than it should be, and 4x more dull. Her attendance policy is very strict, so don't miss any class or your grade will suffer immensely. She did not post a grade the entire semester. Her grading system is also very opaque, which makes it difficult to succeed.

Dropped this class during sylly week. Extremely rigid policies: only one excused absence, no exceptions, plus strict rules on tardiness and screen use. For a 9AM class, this felt out of touch. She framed the course as “easy” because there were no closed-note exams. I wouldn't recommend her unless you need the class to graduate.

I thought this class was a free A, but the final caused my grade to drop to a B+. Graded on attendance, 2 reaction papers, and the final, which is a take-home. review_highlights.comment: Prof. Trejo is class. His lectures are interesting, slow-paced (which I prefer) since he goes into necessary details, and engaging. I have been enjoying this class so far, and I highly recommend taking him if you can. The course is theory based and very useful, especially if you care about labor markets.

Probably the best and only good teacher to take for your upper division econ elective. He's a super chill and nice guy and wants everyone to succeed, unlike other teachers that want you to fail (professor sibley). He is very passionate and cares about the well-being of his students.

Lectures are great, all of them are recorded, which is incredibly helpful. He's very knowledgeable about the subject and teaches very well. Your grade will be wholly determined by 4 online, open-note exams (lowest is dropped) and 3 quizzes. Super chill and great class!

Trejo is a great guy and great teacher. Labor economics is an interesting subject with lots of applications in immigration and education. The online, open book, open note exams are chill because you can re-watch lectures during the exam if you get confused. Just keep in mind that your entire grade is based on 55 MCQs so not a lot of room for error.

Professor Trejo's lectures are amazing, the speed and way in which he explains things make it very easy to understand complex subjects. I think the content of Labor Economics is interesting and he gives us readings and podcasts to relate to practical use and real-world examples. Also the course is super light work, only 3 quizzes and 3 exams.

analysis.name: Mark Tsechansky
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 34 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Mark is super funny and cares about his students. Lectures are engaging enough with his sense of humor. If you pay attention in classes or read the slides the quizzes aren't too bad, and all homeworks are done with a partner so join with a friend and its chill

Professor Tsechansky is a genuinely good professor. He's funny and makes lectures enjoyable, even when the content is tough and technical. The course is graded fairly and there are no midterms. I would definitely recommend him to anyone who needs this class.

Highly recommend this course, the prof is funny and encourages participation. It's lecture-heavy, but slides are posted and really descriptive. Reviews for quizzes are posted, definitely watch those as they help a lot. Sample quizzes somewhat mirror the actual quizzes, which have an individual and group portion so you can get points back.

Do yourself a favor and take this class. I truly believe if you give this class any sort of basic attention, you can't help but get an A. All homework assignments you do in a group, you take quizzes in a group, and he gives you great feedback. If you listen in class you will never even need to study. He's also so nice and really funny.

Professor Tsechansky is a great lecturer who really loves the subject. He tends to explain the same concept but will do it in a couple of different ways to help students understand! Really all you have to do to ace this class is actually listen in class, if you do that, there is barely any outside studying and no extra reading. He's awesome :D

analysis.name: Trent Wintermeier
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Wintermeier is very evidently passionate about his job and wants his students to be passionate too. He inspires good thought and extra-class connections. My only caveat with his class is my own poor attendance and, by policy, missing 4 classes means automatic failure. However, I always felt motivated to go to his class.

Trent is the best!! Take this class!!!

Classes are so chill, and he makes Rhetoric pretty interesting. There are 4 projects over the semester, but they are simple enough, and he does set aside time (instead of 1 class that week) for individual conferencing. There are some short writing assignments, but nothing that should seriously overwhelm you.

the course is structured to be really lenient, assignments are complete/incomplete. the class is super flexible and trent is really caring, he is super nice and i like how the class doesn't punish you for not being an amazing writer but still challenges you.
Love this class. Trent is super caring about his students and makes the course fun and interactive. Couldn't recommend it enough. Just show up, do your work, and you'll get an A.
He was very patient with my class and is honestly very straight forward in the assignments given. The late policy is a bit much though it is at mostly my fault.

analysis.name: Philip Treisman
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 122 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Not gonna lie, you have to work in this class. But if you really want to learn calculus, this man is a God. He knows what he's doing and is willing to help you out with your overall grade through tons of extra credit assignments. This hero broke 3 ribs, came back the next week, AND gave us extra bonus points while continuing to teach. He's the GOAT

This class contains lots of challenging homework. Every week you will have a pop quiz on a topic you learned or should've read about. The tests are really tough. I really struggled at first, despite success in my previous calc class. I HIGHLY RECOMMEND A TUTOR EARLY ON. Do all the extra credit you can, and go to ALL review sessions that he holds.

I took Calculus before & did great, but this is the WORST & challenging class I've ever taken. Do not take this class if you're not an engineering major. The professor tries to be all hilarious & funny, but he's only doing that the first few weeks to not make you want to drop the class. Calculus is already hard, but PROVING calculus is HARDER.

I came in thinking I knew calculus, but his class was very difficult and consumed most of my time throughout the week. Don't get me wrong, as a person Treisman is super nice and the godfather of calculus, but his exams are hard and the combination of quest and textbook homework is impossible to do by yourself so get to know your classmates.

The first day of class is terrifying mostly because you see what Dr. T expects of you and feel you are incapable. I come from a really small town with barely any calculus experience. I worked really  hard (Do the extra credit) but if you get to know him and tell him your story, he's there to help! Prof T is the best calc professor you could have.

analysis.name: Thomas Underwood
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Super awesome guy, very easy to understand, presents material in a great way. Exams are solid, no curve balls. Homeworks are a bit time-consuming most of the time, but other than that - no complaints.

Underwood is a great lecturer and helps one understand the big picture well... however all I learned IS the big picture. Not much time is spent on the specifics, which hurt when doing graded HWs. TAs are helpful but can sometimes be lost as well. Tests are hard but are graded generously. All in all, with lots of  effort, a good grade is possible
Amazing lecturer - low-speed may be challenging and a lot of work, and he not only makes the subject approachable, but also very interesting.
Great professor. I enjoyed his class a lot and he taught complicated material extremely well.

Perfect lectures. Fair assignments that were difficult enough to learn material but were far from overwhelming. Highly flexible. Available outside of class to an extent that went above the norm and beyond expectations. Proud UT engineering has this quality of professors.

analysis.name: Delgerjargal Uvsh
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I took Dr. Uvsh's UGS about corruption. From the start, the rubric for the class was clear and grading was very chill. There are readings weekly but you lowkey don't *have* to do them to do well 😅. Overall, Dr. Uvsh is very nice and lectures are informative and relevant. She also responds to emails quickly. The TA, Vahan, was also great.

She was a super sweet professor who was always available for questions. She gave you a lot of creative freedom which I appreciated and allowed you to research what you were interested in. There was only one mid-term and it was super fair. Additionally the TA was super sweet and helpful. Overall she was a great professor.

Assigned a lot of readings, can do perfectly fine without doing them. Took feedback mid-semester and immediately applied it, reduced readings. Midterm exam was easy, have access to all slides and notes. Great class for the UGS credit and easy A.
There were a lot of readings but not super necessary to do. The only exam is open note, open PowerPoint. Most things can be completed pretty quickly, and she is flexible with deadlines.

Dr. Uvsh was very caring. She did a good job at creating an engaging curriculum. It is helpful to do your notes on the slides she posts for lecture. She made the class structured but not too stressful.

analysis.name: Sean Upshaw
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Super kind, positive, and laid back guy! If you're not great with computer stuff the assignments can be a little hard, but he's such a chill grader that you just have to focus on getting them turned in on time. He's also very understanding if you email him about certain circumstances. Very glad I took this class with Upshaw, highly recommend.

guys trust, take this class. Professor Upshaw is super kind and this class is genuinely the most enjoyable class I've ever taken. The work is very simple and just follow the tutorials in the textbook and that's it. The books have videos embedded so actual reading is minimal. Pretty self paced but you do have to manage your time.
Very kind. He grades easy and has interesting material. Would take his class again.

For ADV 378: Shawn facilitated so many great discussions and had awesome guest lectures and I learned so much about Black advertising. Most lectures were straight from book material which was sometimes hard to focus on, but I feel like the greatest part of this course was the in-class conversations. He was so kind and truly cared about the course.

He graded very easily (I'm pretty sure everyone made an A) and the content was very interesting and easy to understand because of how he structured the course. He would go over the textbook readings in class by formatting the important details into a PowerPoint. He also had some guest speakers who were all great to listen to!

analysis.name: Ulrich Mueller
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Mueller is such a nice professor! Exams are based on lecture and are relatively straight forward. Attendance isn't mandatory but all the exam content is from the lectures so it helps to go. He offers lecture outlines and practice exams. He really wants to help your grade, so I def recommend him! Discussions are chill as long as you talk once.

love dr. mueller. he outlined everything on the exams early and was receptive to feedback. he truly cares about students as it showed he wanted us to succeed. his exams are not hard as long as you study. evolution is def a very interesting topics as is my fav class at ut so far. his exams gave cushion and room to do bad on the quizzes later.

Dr. Mueller is a great professor and truly wants to help you learn. We had two exams in the first half of the class. Study the lecture handouts because all the answers you need are there. Attendance is mandatory for discussion, but show up because it will help your grade. I recommend taking Mueller & Mats for INB 370!

His lectures were very straightforward. He provided a real-life example of almost every concept he taught. He provides a lecture outline that is where 90% of the test questions come from. Discussion is mandatory, but it helps with the reading questions on the test. There are only two grades for this half of the semester, and they are two exams.

Amazing professor! Attendance is not required for lecture, but is required for discussion. Exams are very very easy with multiple choice questions and True/False. He gives out lecture outlines and as long as you study those (know them by heart), you should get an A in his portion of evolution.

analysis.name: Luis Urrieta
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This class is strange. Required to go off campus for field studies as well as you have autonomy to create your own field study, which can turn out to be  annoying as it is highly time consuming at times. You have to go to every class, sick or not in order to do well in this class as well as he isn't very available outside of class. Glad its over!!

Qualitative Inquiry into Education for Social Change; Professor Urrietta was a fantastic lecturer and discussion facilitator. You have to attend class and participate, but I found the course content very interesting and easy to engage with. He gives you autonomy in defining your field observation project which can turn out to be a lot of fun!

Took this as a 5 week summer class and it was A LOT. So much reading! 7 papers due. 10 volunteer hours & 2 outdoor walks in 100+ degrees. Miserable. Lecture heavy with mandatory participation. Learned a lot but felt as if there was an agenda being pushed. Glad it's over.

analysis.name: Arban Uka

review_highlights.comment: Inspirational the right word to describe him. He makes students wanna do something more with their life. Amazing lecturers definitely yes. He always brings up interesting facts + engages students to give their opinion, even if the topic is not that hot (like differential equations). Would take again though I've graduated.

very smart and hardworking. very fair evaluation. always ready to help students. a bit messy and always busy (cause he finds time for everyone). kind and polite to extreme. sometimes hard to follow his genius track of thoughts :( . Still among best profs ive ever had
He details the math step by step, always.

analysis.name: Jason Urban
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Jason gives great feedback and is a fair grader. You can tell that he takes his work seriously and is truly passionate about what he does. I cannot recommend his class enough!

He is great! He gives really specific and helpful feedback to everyones work, and built a polite atmosphere in the online classroom. The class critique process is always so fun and helpful, and I have produce a lot of works Im proud with for my portfolio. Although the workload I can be a bit overwhelming sometimes, but all projects are fun.

I cannot stress enough how much I loved taking a litho class with Jason, he explained everything clearly and gave thorough demos. He does expect you to have good attendance and to actually work during class but it's worth it in order to understand everything that's taught. Definitely take a print class with him if you can!

He is one of the best professor I ever had! He is Inspirational, reasonable, friendly, caring, approachable, and very helpful. He always give clear guidelines when introducing projects. During discussions, he give you comments that can make you go further and want to go further.
nice funny smart cool guy. would recommend. Advice: don't be slovenly.

analysis.name: Viranga Perera
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 102 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Worst class I've ever taken. He overcomplicates topics which makes them seem harder than they actually are. Doesn't give partial credit back despite some past reviews saying he does. Use the homework to study, but expect to teach most of this class to yourself. I also never took physics before this which I think put me at a disadvantage

His summer course is fast paced, but his lectures are great and he makes sure that students understand the content before moving on. There's a decent amount of hw, but it helps to solidify understanding and to prepare for the exams. Course organization is solid and the teaching team is active on Ed. This is likely the easiest UT physics gets.

It's a lab and the lab is one of the worst designed pieces of education conceived. Its all stats and uncertainty. the whole point of physics is that its not engineering, its ideal, but this class is 5% doing physics concepts and 95% propagating uncertainty. Doesn't make a lick of sense. Perera is great though

Perera is an amazing lecturer, and his class is clearly structured and easy. The midterms are multiple choice and similar in difficulty to homework. If there are bad questions, Perera will drop them to ensure the exams result in grades that reflect the knowledge of the material.

The class is a basic introduction to engineering physics. The topics taught are the same as high school physics with a few changes. Dr. Perera wants his students to succeed and has designed his course so that most students will get A's. Homework will take a bit of time, but it isn't too hard; exams are just like the homework. Best UT Physics Prof!

review_highlights.comment: coe prof that doesn't know how to work canvas. quizzes given out whenever he feels like it. hw is announced randomly in class with limited instructions, yet is graded extremely harsh. prof does not know how to lecture, just mumbles his inner monologue in class. prof is also slightly childish/hard to approach without a getting a sarcastic response.
be careful with his dark magic. Overall, this is a horrible class that I wanted to drop out since day one, but I had no other options to choose.

Initially the course was easy then the content became much more difficult. Lectures were provided online in case students wanted to review them. The final project was one of the more challenging projects I have ever completed, but it was also very interesting. I really like him as a professor and his textbook is great. Would take again! :)
Hard to understand, very old school, and not a good lecturer

Steep learning curve (for me at least) which meant putting in a lot of work outside of class. Lectures and lecture slides are uploaded, and textbook is provided by him, otherwise the course and its structure were confusing
